name: Volpino Italiano
description: The Volpino Italiano is an ancient Italian Spitz-type breed that appears in art dating to 1500 BC, and was a beloved companion of Italian nobility and a favorite of Michelangelo. Almost indistinguishable from the early Pomeranian in appearance, the Volpino nearly went extinct in the mid-20th century, with fewer than five individuals recorded in Italy in the 1960s, before a revival program using dogs found on Italian farms brought the breed back from the brink. This lively, spirited little dog was historically used as a watchdog on farms and in carriages, alerting larger dogs to danger with its loud bark.

conditions_detail.name: Patellar Luxation
conditions_detail.description: Patellar luxation occurs when the kneecap slips out of its normal groove in the femur. It can be medial (inward) or lateral (outward) and ranges from grade 1 (occasional slipping) to grade 4 (permanent dislocation).
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Grade 1-2 cases are managed conservatively with weight control and physiotherapy. Grade 3-4 typically require surgical correction to deepen the patellar groove and realign the tibial crest.
prevention_tips: Breed from dogs with normal patellar scores
Avoid high-impact activities in affected puppies
